evals.name: input-validation

assertions.trap: blockedExtensions is a blocklist approach instead of allowlist
SQL query built with string concatenation
exec() with string template instead of execFile with array args
filename used directly in readFile without realpath+prefix check
eval() used to deserialize cookie data
err.message exposed directly to client

evals.name: xss-client

assertions.trap: innerHTML and dangerouslySetInnerHTML used with user content
no DOMPurify or sanitization before rendering HTML
redirect URL used without protocol validation (could be javascript:)
CSP uses unsafe-inline and wildcard instead of nonce/hash
external script loaded without SRI integrity attribute

evals.name: auth-jwt

assertions.trap: JWT stored in localStorage instead of httpOnly cookie
HS256 used with hardcoded secret for what appears to be a public API
30d expiry is too long, no refresh token rotation
jwt.verify doesn't check iss, aud claims
MD5 used for password hashing instead of bcrypt/Argon2id
DELETE /users/:id has no authorization check
JWT_SECRET hardcoded in source code

evals.name: csrf-headers

assertions.trap: SameSite=lax on state-changing endpoint instead of Strict, no CSRF token
no Origin/Referer header validation on POST
no HSTS, X-Frame-Options, nosniff, Referrer-Policy headers
CORS origin: * with credentials: true
no rate limiting on transfer endpoint
Express app without helmet middleware

evals.name: secrets-infra

assertions.trap: database URL, Stripe key, AWS key hardcoded in source
no .gitignore for .env, no secret scanning mentioned
logging full request body (passwords) and headers (tokens)
console.log with raw data instead of structured audit fields
service-to-service call over HTTP without mTLS
no lockfile committed for dependency pinning

evals.name: file-upload-ssrf

assertions.trap: no size limit, MIME type check, or extension validation on upload
arbitrary URL fetched without allowlist validation
originalname used directly, could contain ../
